items.title: US to deploy elite army brigade to Middle East
items.summary: WASHINGTON: The United States is planning to send some 3,000 soldiers from the elite 82nd Airborne Division to the Middle East to support operations against Iran, US media reported Tuesday. They would join thousands of Marines due to arrive in the Middle East by Friday, the latest deadline set by President Donald Trump for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz, according to the Wall Street Journal. No decision has been made to put troops on the ground, the Journal reported, citing two US officials who said a written order on the deployment was expected in coming hours.

items.title: Cops wary of scammers using smaller platforms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police are wary of scam syndicates migrating to smaller digital platforms following the enforcement of the Online Safety Act (ONSA) on Jan 1. Federal police Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa, however, said syndicates are unlikely to abandon mainstream social media and messaging platforms, as these remain critical for reaching a wider pool of potential victims. Although scammers may explore smaller platforms that fall outside licensing requirements, Rusdi said investigations show syndicates still depend heavily on mainstream platforms for initial contact with victims.

items.title: 10pc of Semak Mule queries show positive scam hits
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: About one in 10 queries on the police's Semak Mule portal has returned positive for bank accounts linked to scammers. The Semak Mule portal, which was launched on Oct 7, 2020, allows the public to verify bank accounts, phone numbers and companies before making financial transactions, helping potential victims avoid falling prey to scam syndicates. Apart from the mule accounts, the system also has in its database 251,484 phone numbers and 9,845 companies linked to commercial crime investigations.

items.title: Industry leader suspects insider involvement in spate of burial urn thefts
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The recent thefts of urns from columbaria in several states could be the work of "insiders", an industry player said. Federation of Chinese Cemetery Associations Malaysia chairman Lee Koon Lam said the nature of the thefts suggests they were carefully planned with inside help. The recent incidents include: THE theft of 22 urns from the Perpetual Memorial Park in Kulai, Johor, on March 9.

items.title: Consumer groups want clarity on petrol rules
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Consumer groups have urged the government to clarify guidelines and exemptions following a warning against pumping fuel into containers without a permit. The Federation of Malaysian Consumers Associations (Fomca) said clear and practical rules were necessary to ensure enforcement did not burden consumers. Fomca chief executive officer Dr T. Saravanan said the association agreed with restricting fuel use to vehicles to ensure supply stability.

items.title: Israel set to take 'control' of security zone in south Lebanon
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israel said on Tuesday that its military would take control of south Lebanon up to the Litani River, around 30 kilometres (20 miles) from the border, as deadly strikes pounded the country. In the latest unprecedented step by Lebanese authorities since a new war erupted between Israel and Hizbullah, Beirut's foreign ministry declared the Iranian ambassador persona non grata, giving him until Sunday to leave the country. The Israeli military said that overnight its forces "struck Hizbullah infrastructure in Beirut and in additional areas in Lebanon."

items.title: Calls grow for firecrackers ban as safety concerns mount
items.summary: Consumers' Association of Penang (CAP) president Mohideen Abdul Kader said the government should not have allowed the sale of firecrackers and fireworks in the first place. This creates opportunities for illegal sales to proliferate," he said. Mohideen said CAP had been calling for a total ban on the sale of firecrackers and fireworks for decades.

items.title: Nearly 30,000 eels lost — but this Kelantan duo turned failure into a farming win
items.summary: TUMPAT, March 25 — Two friends who embarked on an eel farming venture five years ago initially suffered huge losses when nearly 30,000 eels died due to disease. Muhammad Azman Abd Rashid @ Ramli and Muhamad Hairi Ishak today run a thriving eel farm on a 0.4-hectare site in Kampung Banggol Petani here, having shifted from farming wild eels to hybrid species. “I have loved eels since I was a child.

items.title: Can locally grown/produced food cushion Malaysia from global shocks caused by the US-Iran conflict?
items.summary: While the country is unlikely to face immediate shortages, the bigger impact lies in rising costs, as Malaysia remains reliant on global markets for key staples and agricultural inputs that underpin even locally produced food. However, Malaysia is also known for its local produce. What food is actually ‘local’?

items.title: Russia fired almost 1,000 drones at Ukraine over last 24 hours - Kyiv air force
items.summary: KYIV: Russia fired nearly 1,000 drones at Ukraine in the past 24 hours, the Ukrainian air force said on Tuesday, after an unprecedented daytime barrage killed at least three people across the war-torn country. "Taking into account the night attack... the enemy used almost 1,000 strike drones," Ukraine's air force said on Telegram, adding that "the geography of the attack during the day was wider than at night" targeting Ukraine's western regions, hundreds of kilometres from the front. - AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: 'Action taken against smoking officers'
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Action has been taken against Health Ministry officers caught smoking at a restaurant recently, said Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. His statement followed a report that the Health Ministry had issued a compound to its enforcement officers for violating the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act. Public health advocates lauded the action against the officers, saying the move built public trust.

items.title: 2 RTD officers probed over viral incidents
items.summary: IPOH: The Road Transport Department (RTD) has suspended two officers as authorities investigate a traffic violation and fatal crash involving a department vehicle. RTD director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli said the cases occurred at different times, but involved the same vehicle. Aedy Fadly said one video showed the vehicle overtaking across a double line and the other showed a fatal collision with a car in Kelantan.

items.title: Goldman Sachs slashes India's economic growth forecast
items.summary: MUMBAI: Goldman Sachs has pared its growth estimate for India this year, while forecasting a 50 basis points (bps) hike in policy rates as the South Asian economy contends with sharp depreciation in its currency. Goldman Sachs forecasts the Indian economy will grow by 5.9 per cent in 2026, compared to its pre-Iran war forecast of seven per cent, it said in a report yesterday. The Wall Street bank had cut its growth forecast for the South Asian economy to 6.5 per cent on March 13.

items.title: HK cops get power to obtain phone, computer passwords
items.summary: HONG KONG: Hong Kong police can now demand that people suspected of breaching the city's national security law to provide mobile phone or computer passwords, in a further crackdown on dissent. The Hong Kong government on Monday used its powers to bypass the city's legislature to gazette the new rules in the national security law, which was imposed by Beijing in 2020. The government said it will brief reporters on the new rules.

items.title: Govt prepares for economic impact from prolonged Middle East conflict
items.summary: The National Security Council (NSC) said the government would adopt a comprehensive and coordinated approach to manage the situation. The special meeting, chaired by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim, would be followed by a special National Economic Action Council (MTEN) meeting tomorrow to deliberate further measures. In addition, NSC said that Malaysia would continue to play a proactive role in contributing towards resolving the conflict by maintaining close relations with international counterparts.

items.title: Firemen save more than 40 premises, school from bush fire
items.summary: PASIR MAS: The Fire and Rescue Department saved more than 40 premises, including a school, after containing a bush and grass fire that spread over about four hectares in Kampung Lati today. "The fire involved bush and dry grass covering an estimated four hectares. Meanwhile, resident Mohd Hairi Ahmad said the fire had spread rapidly towards the end of the road, threatening several housing areas.

items.title: Petrol dealers urge govt cooperation to ease RON95 liability
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Petroleum Dealers Association of Malaysia (PDAM) has called for stronger strategic cooperation between the government and petrol station operators to reduce liability burdens on station owners. The association said reliance solely on manual controls at station level has limitations, particularly in monitoring and preventing misuse of subsidised fuel. "However, we hope that more strategic cooperation can be established between the government and station operators to reduce the liability burden on petrol stations, given the constraints of manual enforcement on the ground," PDAM said in a statement today.

items.title: Firefighters, police search for elderly man, grandson missing in Keningau river
items.summary: KENINGAU: An elderly man and his seven-year-old grandson were reported missing in a river near Taman Bersatu here today. Keningau Fire and Rescue Station chief, Senior Fire Officer Grade B Severinus @ Mohd Nidzam Sainkui, said a nine-member team was deployed immediately after receiving the alert and arrived at the site at 6.20pm. "The team gathered information on the scene before commencing a search and rescue (SAR) operation along the riverbank.

items.title: LLM reports heavy traffic on city-bound routes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Traffic on several major highways in the country began to build tonight as people returned to the capital after the Aidilfitri holidays. A Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) spokesperson said northbound congestion was worst from Gopeng to Tapah and Bidor following an accident. For motorists heading to Johor, traffic congestion was reported between Simpang Renggam and Sedenak.

items.title: Eight-year-old boy drowns in river on first visit
items.summary: SIK: An eight-year-old boy drowned in Sungai Muda at Kampung Tanjung Jeneri near Sik today. Sik district police deputy chief, Deputy Superintendent Safuan Md Noor, said the victim, Muhammad Fatah Saiful, a pupil of SK Kota Bukit, had left his home at about 2pm to cycle with friends. No physical injuries were found on the victim's body, and police have ruled out foul play," he said.

items.title: Today's news in pictures – Tuesday, March 24, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? [[nid:1403202]] KUALA LUMPUR – Children enjoy a dip at the KLCC Water Park to cool off as the country faces a heatwave, with daily maximum temperatures soaring between 37°C and 40°C. NSTP/MOHD FADLI HAMZAH [[nid:1403200]] BALIK PULAU – Farmer Saad Hashim, 73, also known as Pak Awang, gestures toward his dried-up paddy crops at Jalan Sungai Nipah following the prolonged dry spell.

items.title: Dr Dzul honours organ donor Muhammad Zahran in heartfelt tribute
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad paid tribute to late Muhammad Zahran Mohd Zuraidi, who donated his organs to save lives after passing away at Hospital Selayang this morning. She is a true symbol of 'My Family, Respect My Pledge'. Al-Fatihah," he said.

items.title: Azam urges MACC staff to remain steadfast amid criticism
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki has reminded commission staff to always be prepared to face criticism, negative perceptions, and false accusations while performing their duties. He said there would always be parties uncomfortable with MACC's firmness in enforcing the law. Azam said that it is crucial for all MACC officers to uphold the highest level of integrity in every action.

items.title: Malaysia Education Blueprint 2026–2036 aims to reduce teachers' workload
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Education Ministry (MOE) remains committed to implementing initiatives to ease teachers' workload, as outlined in the Malaysia Education Blueprint (RPM) 2026–2035. Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek said the RPM 2026–2035 prioritises ensuring teachers can fully focus on teaching and learning (PdP). Since 2023, several measures have been implemented to reduce teachers' workload, including appointing 20,997 volunteers for the Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia (SPM) examinations, introducing the Malaysia Short-Term Employment Programme (MyStep) for teacher assistants, and assigning full-time hostel warden assistants.

items.title: Malaysia urges peaceful resolution to Middle East conflict
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia maintained its stance that all disputes in the Middle East must be resolved through peaceful means to ease escalating tensions in the region, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said this in a Facebook post after holding telephone conversations with the Crown Prince and Prime Minister of Bahrain, Prince Salman Hamad Al Khalifa, and the President of the United Arab Emirates (UAE), Sheikh Mohamed Zayed Al Nahyan, today. Regional tensions have continued to escalate since the United States and Israel launched joint attacks on Iran on Feb 28.

items.title: Doctor's fight against the Middle East's 'endemic disease' of war
items.summary: During almost three weeks of war in Lebanon, British-Palestinian doctor Ghassan Abu-Sittah has had no respite, saying he has been working "against the clock" to save children wounded in Israeli bombardment. Last week, Israeli strikes hit densely populated central Beirut areas not far from the hospital, with three badly wounded children pulled from the rubble. Lebanese authorities say that 118 children have been killed and 370 others wounded.

items.title: Investors look beyond Iran war
items.summary: THE scale and duration of this month's energy and inflation shock are still guesswork. But global investors are reluctant to throw in the towel on stocks and bonds, despite the Iran war and a dour first quarter for returns. The first is that history suggests these conflicts and related oil supply disruptions are often temporary.

items.title: NST Leader: How the Iran war is harming the world
items.summary: With Teheran turning the Strait of Hormuz into a chokepoint, an attack on Iran has now become an "attack" on the rest of the world. The world is in dire straits, writes an analyst. With Iran closing the strait to ships, thereby removing about 20 per cent of the world's oil supply, the energy shock has become a full-blown economic one.

items.title: Muslims urged to perform prayers for rain amid hot weather
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA: Muslims are urged to perform Solat Sunat Istisqa' (prayers for rain) following the ongoing hot and dry weather sweeping across the country. Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs), Dr Zulkifli Hasan, said the prayers should be observed continuously as a sign of repentance and reliance on Allah SWT. Yesterday, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad reportedly said that 15 heat-related cases had been recorded so far this year, including three cases of heatstroke and one death involving a child left in a vehicle.

items.title: 'Has mum been buried?' asks girl after mother dies in crash
items.summary: That was the question asked by Nur Raesha Amanda Mohd Redzuan, 14, after she was informed by family members that her mother, Nurul Hafizah Shaary, 39, had died in an accident yesterday. Nur Raesha Amanda and her younger sibling, Nur Rakhis Aral, nine, were injured and received treatment at hospital. The children's granduncle, Mohd Tarmizi Said, 42, said Nur Raesha Amanda had asked about her mother early this morning while receiving treatment at Hospital Dungun.

items.title: Police detain three men over armed rioting in Kuala Langat
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: Police have detained three men suspected of rioting with weapons in Jalan Waja, Telok Panglima Garang, Kuala Langat, near here yesterday. Kuala Langat district police chief, Deputy Superintendent Mohd Sufian Amin, said police received a report from a man regarding the incident at 2.51pm. Mohd Sufian said police later detained three men believed to be involved in the incident following investigations and intelligence gathering.

items.title: Three-year-old girl dies after being found unconscious in pond
items.summary: IPOH: A three-year-old girl died after she was found unconscious in a drainage pond near her home in Simpang 4 Permatang Pelandok, Teluk Intan, yesterday. Hilir Perak district police chief, Assistant Commissioner Chua Kok Lian, said the incident was reported to police at 9.24pm. "Initial investigations indicated the incident occurred at 7pm, when the child was found unconscious in the pond.

items.title: From sunburn to stroke — experts warn heatwave packs more than discomfort
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The heatwave currently affecting the country not only increases health risks but may also lead to long-term physiological complications such as heatstroke. Universiti Putra Malaysia (UPM) public health medicine expert, Associate Prof Dr Malina Osman said health risks from hot weather can be divided into two main categories: acute effects and long-term consequences. “This condition typically affects individuals unaccustomed to heat or those suddenly exposed to high temperatures.

items.title: Mohamad Sabu: Malaysia steps up food security measures as West Asia conflict and hot weather bite
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The Ministry of Agriculture and Food Security (KPKM), together with relevant agencies, has stepped up preparedness efforts to safeguard the nation’s food security amid ongoing geopolitical conflict in West Asia and the ongoing hot weather. He said that uncertainties in the global landscape resulting from the geopolitical conflict in West Asia could affect the global supply chain with higher oil prices and logistics costs ultimately impacting food prices. Therefore, Mohamad assured that essential food supplies for this year’s Aidilfitri celebration would remain stable, sufficient, and accessible at competitive prices.

items.title: Hornbill crashes into building as bush fire haze blankets Miri, firefighters race to save protected bird
items.summary: MIRI, March 24 — The Fire and Rescue Department (Bomba) rescued a critically injured hornbill found on the ground near a building in the Kuala Baram Industrial Estate, as Miri experienced sweltering heat and bush fires in Lutong on Tuesday. The distressed bird, a protected species, was discovered by members of the public near Bangunan Daiken Miri Sdn Bhd, who promptly alerted Bomba. Bomba Miri personnel responded to the call and found the hornbill in a weakened state and sent it to Piasau Nature Reserve (PNR) at Pulau Melayu for further treatment and care, said Bomba Lutong Station chief Henry Jugah.

items.title: Student facing court charges over Quran desecration and offensive Threads posts expelled from university
items.summary: KUANTAN, March 24 — A student at Universiti Malaysia Pahang Al-Sultan Abdullah (UMPSA), charged with desecrating the Quran and posting offensive content on social media, has been expelled from the university. UMPSA vice-chancellor, Prof Dr Yatimah Alias, said the male student’s disciplinary case was recently closed after the Student Disciplinary Committee met on March 9. “Following disciplinary proceedings, the student was found guilty and expelled under Rule 58 of the Universiti and University College (UMPSA) (Student Discipline) Rules 2024,” she said in a statement today.

items.title: Aidilfitri amid the flames: Kota Tinggi firefighters battle 150-hectare blaze through festive season
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, March 24 — While millions across the country are celebrating Aidilfitri, the orange-clad heroes from the Punggai Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) here are spending the festive season amid intense heat and thick plumes of smoke. Since March 21, they have been working tirelessly to extinguish a 150-hectare bush fire along the Punggai Bypass Highway heading towards Sebana Cove, which is now entering its fourth day. For auxiliary fire officer Iz’aan Nazmi Suri, 24, this year’s celebration carries special meaning as he recently got married in January.

items.title: Highway jams stretch north, south and east as Klang Valley-bound motorists make their way home
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Traffic flow on several major expressways nationwide showed an increasing trend, with slow-moving traffic reported following a rise in the number of vehicles heading towards the Klang Valley as of 4pm today. Malaysian Highway Authority (LLM) Traffic Management Centre supervisor Muhammad Aliff Syafiq Md Takiyudin said congestion was detected on routes heading north, south and the east coast. Commenting on traffic disruptions, Muhammad Aliff Syafiq said several incidents involving broken-down vehicles had contributed to congestion, including in the northern sector after the Menora Tunnel, heading towards the city centre, which caused a traffic build-up of about seven kilometres (km).

items.title: No shortage here: Armizan says petrol, diesel and LPG supply steady as authorities step up monitoring
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 24 — The current supply of petrol, diesel and liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) in the country remains stable, with no shortages reported so far, says Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali. “These issues have been addressed, and continuous monitoring is being carried out to ensure smooth delivery of supplies,” he said in a Facebook post today after paying a courtesy call on Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor. He said claims of insufficient fuel supply at several petrol stations in Sabah recently were also being linked to supply disruption situations in neighbouring countries, and therefore urged the public not to engage in panic buying, as supplies were sufficient.

items.title: Indira Gandhi’s ex-husband’s Budi95 petrol subsidy account still active; lawyers ask why not suspended
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Lawyers for Hindu mother M. Indira Gandhi highlighted that the Budi95 petrol subsidy account of her ex-husband K. Patmanathan is still active even until today, even as he remains a wanted man after he took their daughter away. Indira’s lawyers Rajesh Nagarajan and Sachpreetraj Singh said it is publicly known that the Malaysian authorities have been aware since November 2025 that Patmanathan has been and continues to be eligible for the Budi95 subsidy. Lawyers for Hindu mother M. Indira Gandhi highlighted that the Budi95 petrol subsidy account of her ex-husband K. Patmanathan is still active.

items.title: Anwar: Malaysia’s local currency trade with China, Thailand and Indonesia surges to RM82b, cutting reliance on US dollar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Malaysia recorded RM82.1 billion in trade and investment payments with China, Thailand and Indonesia settled in local currencies as at November 2025, a sharp rise from just RM3.6 billion in 2009. Anwar said Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) has taken proactive steps to promote local currency use in cross-border trade, reducing exposure to exchange rate volatility and strengthening financial resilience. “The government and BNM will continue to strengthen and diversify local currency settlement channels to facilitate trade and investment, supporting the sustainability of the regional financial system,” Anwar said.

items.title: Heatwave fails to slow Penang’s holiday buzz as tourists flock to heritage streets and seaside spots
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 24 — Scorching temperatures in Penang, particularly in the Timur Laut district, have done little to deter tourists, with popular attractions continuing to draw strong crowds during the Hari Raya and school holiday period. Despite the hot weather, these locations remained lively, with tourists strolling, taking photos and enjoying their time in Penang. Wang Fang, 30, from China, said the warm weather had not affected her travel experience, describing Penang as a tourist-friendly destination.

items.title: Fun in the sun? Perlis at 40°C reshapes 2026 Aidilfitri celebrations for Chuping residents
items.summary: PADANG BESAR, March 24 — The searing heat in Chuping, Perlis has locals staying home and avoiding the midday sun, choosing to go out only for the essentials instead of visiting each others’ homes for open house parties this festive season. With temperatures rising to 40°Celsius, celebrations this Raya Puasa are hitting residents hard, Berita Harian reported today. Housewife Nur Hafsah Awalluddin, 39, said the extreme weather forced her to take extra care for her children.

items.title: MMEA swoops in off Lumut, detains two tankers over suspected illegal oil transfer near Pulau Jarak
items.summary: IPOH, March 24 — The Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) detained two oil tankers suspected of conducting unauthorised ship-to-ship (STS) bunkering activities near Pulau Jarak, Lumut, yesterday. Its Perak director, Captain Mohamad Shukri Khotob, said the vessels were intercepted at 8pm, some 11 nautical miles east of the island. He said both vessels were ordered to anchor 11.5 nautical miles east of Pulau Jarak for handover to investigation officers, with monitoring assisted by the Lumut Sea Surveillance System (Swasla).

items.title: Tengku Zafrul urges Malaysia to seize Gulf capital as investors rethink risk in a turbulent world
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Malaysia must position itself as a credible and compelling alternative as global investors reassess risk amid the crisis in West Asia, said Malaysian Investment Development Authority (Mida) chairman Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. However, Tengku Zafrul said history shows that periods of disruption reorder the global economy, with capital shifting, supply chains realigning and investors reassessing risk — creating strategic openings that Malaysia must be ready to seize. “If investors are reassessing risk, Malaysia must present itself as a credible and compelling alternative.

items.title: Malaysia calls for ceasefire as Anwar and Luxon swap views on West Asia turmoil and its ripple effects on global security
items.summary: In a Facebook post, Anwar said the stance was conveyed during a telephone conversation with New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon, with Malaysia continuing to call for an immediate ceasefire, protection of civilians and a resolution through diplomatic channels. “During a telephone conversation with my counterpart from New Zealand, Prime Minister Christopher Luxon, we exchanged views on developments in West Asia and their impact on global security and the economy,” he said. The Prime Minister also welcomed proactive measures taken by the New Zealand government to strengthen energy security and accelerate the transition to renewable energy.

items.title: DPM Fadillah clarifies Malaysia not facing ‘water bankruptcy’, but must manage rising demand by 2030
items.summary: KUCHING, March 24 — Malaysia is not at risk of facing “water bankruptcy” as the country still has sufficient raw water resources, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Amar Fadillah Yusof. However, he stressed that the real challenge lies in the integrated management of water resources, protection of river basins, and improving the efficiency of the national water delivery system. Fadillah highlighted several key issues requiring attention, including water storage management, encroachment into catchment areas, river pollution, and the lack of integration among alternative water sources such as groundwater, recycled water, rainwater harvesting and desalination.

items.title: No price tags, misleading pricing, unverified scales: Selangor cracks down on rogue traders at festive bazaars in anti-profiteering sweep
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, March 24 — As Malaysians increasingly spend prudently in response to rising living costs, the Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) in Selangor has stepped up enforcement to protect consumers from unfair trading practices. Selangor KPDN director Muhamad Hanif Asa’ari said the ministry’s “Ops Pantau 2026” inspections ran from February 19 and covered 1,284 premises across the state. “Types of offences included failure to display price tags, using unverified weighing instruments and misleading price marking.

items.title: No need for panic buying: Hajiji, Armizan address Sabah fuel concerns amid recent rumours
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 24 — Sabah’s petrol, diesel and LPG supply is stable, and the public is urged not to panic buy, federal Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Minister Datuk Armizan Mohd Ali said today. Armizan debriefed Sabah Chief Minister Datuk Seri Hajiji Noor earlier on the outcomes of the Special Committee on Coordination to Combat Leakage and Smuggling Among Enforcement Agencies, which has been activated to strengthen enforcement efforts against fuel smuggling and misappropriation, particularly in border areas. Armizan added that he also addressed several rumours regarding alleged fuel shortages at some petrol stations in Sabah recently.

items.title: Explainer: How the Middle East conflict and China fertiliser curbs are rattling Malaysia’s vegetable supply
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — Malaysia’s vegetable industry, a critical hub for domestic and regional food security, may be facing what food security experts have described as the “perfect storm” of supply chain disruptions. The ‘60 per cent’ vulnerability Malaysia is quite dependent on the global market for its farming nutrients, importing over 60 per cent of its mineral fertiliser requirements, food security expert Fatimah Mohamed Arshad explained. The channel handles nearly 30 per cent of world oil exports, a fifth of the natural gas supply and one-third of global fertiliser exports.

items.title: Penang water supply stable for at least two months as dams, Sungai Muda above alert level despite heatwave
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 24 — The Penang Water Supply Corporation (PBAPP) today gave an assurance that the state’s water supply will hold steady for at least two months even amid the ongoing heatwave. All three major dams in Penang are also holding healthy levels, with Air Itam Dam at 73.4 per cent, Teluk Bahang Dam at 86 per cent, and Mengkuang Dam at 91.4 per cent. Pathmanathan said controlled drawdown strategies ensure that the state can sustain water supply for months, with Air Itam Dam lasting up to 72 days (about 2.4 months), Mengkuang 132 days (4.4 months), and Teluk Bahang 261 days (8.7 months) under current usage rates.

items.title: A jetty for visitors but not villagers? DBKK urged to open new facility to everyday commuters
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, March 24 — Kota Kinabalu City Hall (DBKK) must open its newly launched tourism jetty for public use, following growing concerns over the safety of existing facilities relied upon daily by Pulau Gaya residents. Tanjung Aru assemblyman Datuk Junz Wong said the situation faced by islanders commuting to and from the city reflected a harsh reality, with many forced to use ageing, wet, and unsafe jetties. Wong noted that the new DBKK Tourist Jetty under DBKK Holdings is equipped with modern and more comfortable facilities but designated solely for tourism purposes.

items.title: Fire extinguishers, safety courses: Johor plans tougher rules for fireworks traders after Kampung Dato' Sulaiman Menteri blaze
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 24 — The Johor government is tightening the rules on the sale of fireworks after a fire broke out at a stall selling them in Kampung Dato’ Sulaiman Menteri here last Sunday that damaged two vehicles nearby. State Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor said the proposals include making it mandatory for traders to have safety equipment such as fire extinguishers and to maintain a safe distance from public gathering areas. “The state government sees the need to tighten its existing conditions, including setting sales locations further away from public areas and reviewing the amount and type of firecrackers and fireworks sold.

items.title: DBKL to pay parents RM824,180 after fallen tree kills motorcyclist-son, court finds negligence
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, March 24 — The Sessions Court here has ordered the Kuala Lumpur Mayor to pay RM824,180 in damages to a couple who lost their son after he was killed by a fallen tree on a road divider maintained by Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL). In his grounds of judgment dated March 9, 2026, Effandi Nazila held that the fallen tree was under DBKL’s care and that the incident would not have occurred in the absence of negligence in its monitoring and maintenance. “The tree that fell was under DBKL’s maintenance, and its responsibility is therefore clear and established.

items.title: After child falls, Johor to replace guardrails at Desa Mutiara PPR, inspect 15,000 public low-cost flats
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, March 24 — The Johor government is budgeting RM3 million to replace all guardrails at the Desa Mutiara People’s Housing Project (PPR) here, after a boy fell from a second floor unit and suffered heavy injuries. State Housing and Local Government Committee chairman Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor the move involves some 2,000 guardrails and is necessary for the safety of residents. Mohd Jafni said the special allocation is from the state-owned Johor Housing Development Corporation.

items.title: Repeat offender with 13 convictions nabbed within hours over Kulim shooting, motive still unclear
items.summary: KULIM, March 24 — The suspect in the shooting at Kampung Permatang Durian, Seberang Paya, Padang Serai on March 21 has 13 prior criminal and drug-related convictions. Kulim deputy police chief DSP Tengku Mohd Faisal Tengku Yeng said the 42-year-old lorry driver was arrested within 24 hours of the incident at a house in Kampung Naga Lilit, Padang Serai, at 12.30am. However, he tested negative for drugs,” he said at a press conference at Kulim district police headquarters today.

items.title: Firefighters contain bush fire in Tatau near Bintulu after second day of operations
items.summary: BINTULU, March 24 — The Tatau Fire and Rescue Station (BBP) team prevented a bush fire from spreading further in an area covering about 1.62 hectares along the Kuala Tatau-Serupai coastal stretch in Tatau today. The Sarawak Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) Operations Centre, in a statement, said that the recurring fire was brought under control on the second day of the operation. Seven personnel were involved in the operation, supported by one Light Fire Rescue Tender (LFRT) and four-wheel-drive vehicles.

items.title: Anwar backs Indonesia’s bid to cool Iran-Israel conflict, calls for talks and civilian protection
items.summary: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said such support for Jakarta’s initiative is expected to help open the door to meaningful negotiations towards a peaceful and lasting solution in West Asia. In a Facebook post, Anwar said the matter was among the issues discussed during his telephone conversation with Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto yesterday. “We both called for peace, and I informed President Prabowo that Malaysia supports all efforts aimed at achieving peace and resolving the conflict,” he said.

items.title: Three Malaysians among 49 arrested in Phnom Penh raid on global online scam syndicate
items.summary: PHNOM PENH, March 24 — Cambodian authorities have arrested three Malaysians among the 49 suspected members of an online scam syndicate and seized 46 Malaysian SIM cards during a raid in the capital on Sunday. State media Agence Kampuchea Presse reported that the group allegedly used Cambodia as its operational base to target Malaysians and victims in various countries using fake investment schemes. “Authorities said those detained included 43 Chinese nationals, three Malaysians, one Vietnamese woman, one Myanmar woman and one Cambodian woman,” reported the state media this morning.

items.title: Disturbing footage of indecent act in Penang cemetery sparks police probe, two now in custody
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, March 24 — Police have arrested two individuals suspected of engaging in an indecent act at a cemetery in Batu Gantung, Air Itam, after a video of the incident went viral on social media. Timur Laut police chief ACP Abdul Rozak Muhammad said a report was received from a 69-year-old man about the video, which was recorded at a Chinese cemetery. “The first suspect, a 37-year-old woman, was arrested yesterday around 8.35pm at a flat near Jalan Padang Tembak.
